resultRows.description: The public Heart Health Genetic Guide sample row appears in the antiplatelet section with ITGB3 and Article source labeling.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample-report row for Abciximab. Use this as Heart Health Genetic Guide output-format evidence only; it is not a local medication recommendation, safety conclusion, efficacy prediction, dose instruction, or treatment-change instruction.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: CYP2C19: Reduced clopidogrel active metabolite formation; increased on-treatment platelet reactivity; increased risk for adverse cardiac and cerebrovascular events
resultRows.description: The sample row shows a CPIC-labeled CYP2C19 result for clopidogrel in the antiplatelet section.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample-report row for Clopidogrel. Use this as Heart Health Genetic Guide output-format evidence only; it is not a local medication recommendation, safety conclusion, efficacy prediction, dose instruction, or treatment-change instruction.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: Predicted to have increased hydralazine plasma concentrations compared to NAT2 rapid and intermediate metabolizers, which may lead to both increased efficacy and adverse effects, including drug-induced systemic lupus erythematosus.
resultRows.description: The sample row shows a CPIC-labeled NAT2 antianginal result with a multi-line narrative result field.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed sample-report row for Hydralazine. Use this as Heart Health Genetic Guide output-format evidence only; it is not a local medication recommendation, safety conclusion, efficacy prediction, dose instruction, or treatment-change instruction.

clinicianReview: Use this report as a structured discussion aid only; a clinician or pharmacist must review heart medication questions with the full medication list and clinical context.
Do not start, stop, switch, rank, or dose heart medications from this local output or from sample rows.
If a medication decision is being considered, confirm pharmacogene calls with clinically appropriate testing and professional interpretation.

references.title: FDA Table of Pharmacogenomic Biomarkers in Drug Labeling
Heart Health Genetic Guide sample report

genotypeSummary.effect: One no function allele and one normal function allele
One decreased function allele and one normal function allele
One no function allele and one normal function allele
One increased function allele and one no function allele
Synonymous and missense variant rows are visible in the sample appendix.
The public sample appendix lists exploratory functional-assessment rows and normal-function no-variant rows for additional cardiovascular PGx genes.
genotypeSummary.genotype: ACE rs4343 A/A; ADRB1 rs1801252 A/A; ADRB2 rs1042713 G/G, rs2400707 A/G, rs275651 T/T; AGTR1 rs5186 A/C; APOB rs679899 G/G; CES1 rs2244613 T/T, rs2244614 A/A, rs8192950 G/G; ITGB3 rs5918 C/C; KCNH2 rs1137617 A/G
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Altered function or normal function as shown in the sample appendix
genotypeSummary.variantId: selected exploratory rows visible in appendix

uncertainty: No calibrated medication-response, adverse-event, bleeding, clotting, myopathy, cardiovascular-event, diagnosis, or dosing probability model is supplied.
Probability, confidence, calibration status, and missing-data limits stay in the appendix only.
missingInputs: Current medication list, indication, dose, treatment history, renal or liver function, drug interactions, cardiovascular diagnosis, clinician review, phenotype confidence, and medication-decision context unavailable in this fixture

User-specific ABCG2, CYP2C9, CYP3A4, CYP3A5, G6PD, HLA-B, NAT2, VKORC1, ABCB1, ACE, ADRB1/2, AGTR1, APOB, APOE, CES1, ITGB3, KCNH2, and additional sample-table genes are unavailable unless supplied by a validated local PGx caller
limitations: The public sample PDF is observed output structure, not local medication advice.
The sample PDF says the report is not FDA or EMA reviewed and is not intended for diagnosis, treatment, cure, prevention, medication appropriateness, or medical advice.
Pharmacogenomics evidence is time-bound and may change as research changes; the local output is not automatically updated.
Variant coverage can be incomplete and can be limited by DNA-test quality, private variants, pseudogenes, similar sequences, and genotyping-vs-WGS coverage.
Medication decisions require qualified clinician or pharmacist review with the full clinical and medication context.
